daily_brief: Sunday's global news was dominated by the arrival of the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius in Tenerife, where Spanish authorities began an unprecedented 23-country evacuation operation after three passengers died from the rare Andes strain [BBC, RFI, France 24, Al Jazeera]. In Hungary, Péter Magyar was sworn in as prime minister, marking the end of Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule and signaling a decisive pro-EU shift as the European flag was reinstated on parliament [RFI, BBC, Le Monde]. Russian President Vladimir Putin declared the Ukraine conflict is 'coming to an end' following a scaled-back Victory Day parade in Moscow, though both sides accused each other of violating a US-brokered ceasefire [BBC, France 24]. Tensions in the Middle East remained elevated as Israeli strikes killed at least 39 people in Lebanon despite the ceasefire, while Iran's Revolutionary Guards threatened US sites in the region if Iranian tankers come under fire [BBC, France 24, The Hindu]. In the Sahel, jihadist attacks in central Mali killed over 70 people in recent days, with Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM intensifying assaults on villages [France 24, The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Arrives in Tenerife as Spain Launches Unprecedented Evacuation
bullets: The Dutch-flagged MV Hondius arrived at the port of Granadilla in Tenerife early Sunday after weeks at sea, carrying nearly 150 passengers and crew.
Three passengers have died from the Andes strain of hantavirus—a Dutch couple and a German woman—with at least eight total cases confirmed or suspected.
WHO officials classified all aboard as 'high-risk contacts' while emphasizing the risk to the general public remains low; WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus traveled to Spain to oversee operations.
The evacuation involves 23 countries coordinating repatriation flights, with the final flight to Australia scheduled for Monday.

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the 'unprecedented' nature of the operation and local concerns from Canary Islands officials

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on WHO statements and international coordination

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Similar coverage to RFI with additional detail on evacuation timeline

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Included WHO chief's open letter to Tenerife residents

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Earlier reporting focused on British nationals affected

top_stories.headline: Péter Magyar Sworn In as Hungary's Prime Minister, Ending Orbán's 16-Year Rule
bullets: Péter Magyar took the oath of office Saturday after his Tisza party won 141 of 199 parliamentary seats in April elections, rising from zero seats just two years ago.
The EU flag was reinstated on Hungary's parliament building for the first time in 12 years, symbolizing a pro-European shift.
Magyar declared he would 'serve, not rule over Hungary' and promised 'regime change' while tens of thousands of supporters celebrated outside parliament.
Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party collapsed from 135 to 52 seats, with Orbán and key figures declining to take their parliamentary seats.

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized EU welcome and von der Leyen's positive response

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Focused on domestic political implications and corruption allegations against Fidesz

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article inaccessible but headline indicated celebratory framing

top_stories.headline: Putin Declares Ukraine War 'Coming to an End' as Victory Day Parade Scales Back
bullets: Speaking after a scaled-back Victory Day parade in Moscow, Putin said he believes the conflict with Ukraine is 'coming to an end' while condemning Western support for Kyiv.
The annual parade lacked tanks, missiles, and other military hardware due to security concerns over potential Ukrainian drone attacks.
A US-brokered three-day ceasefire was in effect but both Russia and Ukraine accused each other of violations throughout Saturday.
Putin stated he would only meet Ukrainian President Zelensky once certain conditions are met, without specifying details.

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting both Putin's statements and context of scaled-back parade

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Steve Rosenberg analysis emphasized the diminished nature of this year's event

top_stories.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill at Least 39 in Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
bullets: Lebanese health ministry reported 39 deaths from Israeli strikes on Saturday, including seven people killed in one strike on the town of Saksakiyeh, among them a child.
The IDF stated it was targeting Hezbollah infrastructure and personnel, claiming it took steps to minimize civilian harm including using 'precise munitions.'
Fighting has continued since President Trump announced a ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon on April 16.
Most strikes have concentrated in southern Lebanon, with the IDF saying it targeted 'Hezbollah terrorists operating from within a structure used for military purposes.'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Presented both Lebanese casualty figures and Israeli military statements

top_stories.headline: Jihadist Attacks in Central Mali Kill Over 70 in Days of Violence
bullets: Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM launched attacks on villages in central Mali on Friday, following earlier assaults on Wednesday that killed at least 30 people.
Local officials reported death tolls ranging from 70 to 80 in the recent wave of violence, with one describing the situation as 'our hearts are bleeding.'
Security sources said JNIM is targeting villages that refused to sign local agreements with the armed group.
The attacks follow an unprecedented joint assault by JNIM and the Tuareg-dominated Azawad Liberation Front against Mali's ruling junta last month.

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided context on broader security deterioration and junta's struggles

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article inaccessible but headline confirmed coverage
